====Pokémon====
=====On hand=====

|desc={{p|Gyarados}} was very briefly seen during his presentation, where he was riding on it. It was not used in the Gym match against Platinum.

None of Gyarados's moves are known.}}

{{anchor|Floatzel 2}}{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Crasher Wake
|pkmn=Floatzel
|gender=male
|type1=Water
|img=Wake Floatzel Adventures.png
|epnum=PS364
|epname=A Skuffle with Skorupi
|desc={{p|Floatzel}} was the first Pokémon sent against Platinum, going up against her {{TP|Platinum|Empoleon}} in the Gym's pool. Both Pokémon demonstrated great speed, but Floatzel was eventually hit by a {{m|Vacuum Wave}}, sending him flying and defeating him.

He later reappeared with Quagsire in ''[[PS400|Bogging Down Quagsire]]'', where both used Rain Dance to fill up the dried-up [[Lake Valor]].

Floatzel's known moves are {{m|Aqua Jet}} and {{m|Rain Dance}}, and his Ability is {{a|Swift Swim}}.}}

{{anchor|Quagsire 2}}{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Crasher Wake
|gender=male
|pkmn=Quagsire
|type1=Water
|type2=Ground
|img=Wake Quagsire Adventures.png
|epnum=PS364
|epname=A Skuffle with Skorupi
|desc={{p|Quagsire}} was the second and last Pokémon chosen by Wake to go against Platinum and her {{TP|Platinum|Empoleon}}. Both Pokémon started by using Blizzard, and kept using it until Empoleon collapsed from exhaustion. Quagsire next faced {{adv|Diamond}}'s {{p|Torterra}}, [[Tru]]. After tossing it into the pool, Quagsire prepared to attack Tru as soon as he surfaced. However, Platinum surprised Wake by swimming behind him before surfacing, allowing Tru to defeat Quagsire with {{m|Giga Drain}}, thus making Platinum the winner.

Quagsire later reppeared with Floatzel in ''[[PS400|Bogging Down Quagsire]]'', where both used Rain Dance to fill up the dried-up [[Lake Valor]].

Quagsire's known moves are {{m|Blizzard}} and {{m|Rain Dance}}, and his Ability is {{a|Damp}}.}}

=====Status unknown=====

Lumineon's only known move is {{m|Aqua Tail}}.}}
